title: Cousinia refracta
intro: Cousinia refracta is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Aster family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Cousinia refracta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Cousinia refracta include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Cousinia refracta is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Cousinia. More specifically, it is classified as a species within the tribe Cardueae. Cousinia refracta is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it shares characteristics with other flowering plants in this family.
